brief_title: Semaglutide in Obese Adults with Type 2 Diabetes and Elevated HbA1c
official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Dose-Ranging Study of Subcutaneous Semaglutide in Obese Adults with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus and Inadequate Glycemic Control
brief_summary: This Phase 2 dose-ranging study evaluates the efficacy and safety of two doses of subcutaneous semaglutide versus placebo in obese adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus and elevated HbA1c. Co-primary endpoints include change in HbA1c and body weight at 16 weeks.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 30-65 years

2. Documented diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus

3. HbA1c between 8.0% and 11.0% at screening

4. Body mass index (BMI) > 30 kg/m²

5. On stable oral antidiabetic therapy (metformin, sulfonylurea, or combination) for >= 8 weeks

6. Fasting C-peptide >= 0.7 ng/mL

7. eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m²

8. Stable body weight (< 5% change in past 3 months)

9. Ability to self-administer subcutaneous injections

10. Written informed consent obtained

11. Willingness to adhere to study visit schedule
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Type 1 diabetes mellitus

2. Currently using insulin therapy

3. Prior use of GLP-1 receptor agonist within 6 months

4. History of pancreatitis

5. Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 syndrome

6. History of bariatric surgery

7. Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C)

8. Active malignancy (except non-melanoma skin cancer)

9. Pregnant, breastfeeding, or planning pregnancy during study period

10. Uncontrolled psychiatric illness

inclusion_criteria.text: Documented diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus
On stable oral antidiabetic therapy (metformin, sulfonylurea, or combination) for >= 8 weeks
Stable body weight (< 5% change in past 3 months)
Willingness to adhere to study visit schedule

exclusion_criteria.text: Prior use of GLP-1 receptor agonist within 6 months
Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 syndrome
Pregnant, breastfeeding, or planning pregnancy during study period

sites.facility: All India Institute of Medical Sciences
